decisions.summary: Three beads, one per finding. F1 (delete lifecycle event), F2 (FleetAgentsView NaN guard), F3 (DrawerActiveAgent stream excerpt regression). Each is in a different file/subsystem and independently reviewable.

F1 fix approach: parameterize the lifecycle event name in agentStopRoute(method, path) by adding a third arg `event: 'agent.delete_requested' | 'agent.stop_requested'`. Keeps the factory shared (single source of truth for stop/delete pipeline) but restores observable event distinction. Splitting the routes back into two near-identical handlers would duplicate ~40 lines of identical logic and is the worse trade-off.

F2 fix approach: guard the reduce in FleetAgentsView.tsx:290 with Number.isFinite() on `new Date(agent.startedAt).getTime()` before adding to the accumulator. Also recompute the divisor to count only valid-duration agents so the average is correct (otherwise an empty/invalid agent still divides the sum). Simpler alternative — skip with Number.isFinite() and keep dividing by runningAgents.length — distorts the average downward and isn't worth shipping.

F3 fix approach: investigate first (the component at DrawerActiveAgent.tsx:50 already calls selectAgentOutput). Walk back the data flow: check whether server emits agent.output_received for the affected agents, whether the WS subscription is active, and whether selectAgentOutput's keying matches the actual agent IDs the server uses. Fix the broken link; do NOT add defensive shims downstream. Includes a Playwright e2e regression test.

Every bead ships with a test. F1: server route test asserting the correct lifecycle event string for DELETE vs POST /stop. F2: FleetAgentsView.test.tsx case with one valid + one invalid startedAt asserting finite avgRuntime. F3: Playwright e2e per the issue's stated minimum bar.

hazards.summary: F1: other consumers may be filtering on `agent.stop_requested` and currently catching DELETE events accidentally. Restoring `agent.delete_requested` for DELETE could surface a latent gap in those consumers.
hazards.mitigation: Grep `agent.stop_requested` and `agent.delete_requested` across src/ and packages/ before changing the emit. If a consumer was relying on DELETE-as-stop, decide explicitly (likely: update the consumer to subscribe to both) rather than silently re-aliasing.

hazards.summary: F2: the divisor change (counting only finite-duration agents) is a behavior change beyond a pure guard. With all-invalid running agents the metric should display 0, not NaN or Infinity.
hazards.mitigation: Test covers three cases: all valid, mixed valid/invalid, all invalid. All-invalid case must yield avgRuntime = 0 (matching the existing empty-array branch).

hazards.summary: F3 may have multiple root causes (selector key mismatch, WS subscription not subscribing for this agent, server not emitting agent.output_received for the agent class, projection reducer missing case). The agent must diagnose before changing.
hazards.mitigation: Bead description requires writing the diagnosed root cause into the PR body before the fix. No 'defensive shim downstream' fixes — fix at the broken link only, per CLAUDE.md 'No Bandaids' rule.

hazards.summary: F3's Playwright test must use an isolated browser instance and seed agentOutputById via store.setState or a server mock — it must not depend on a live agent producing output.
hazards.mitigation: Follow the existing pattern in src/dashboard/frontend/tests/*.spec.ts (e.g., command-deck-resource-strip.spec.ts) which stubs the dashboard store directly via page.evaluate.

sessionHistory.note: Initial planning session (auto mode) for PAN-1221 — PAN-1148 follow-up: F1 delete lifecycle event, F2 NaN guard in FleetAgentsView, F3 stream output panel regression
